response_parsed.opinion: Stereotype threat, a term popularized by social psychologists Stacy Spears and Elizabeth Peterson in the early 2000s, refers to the anxious anticipation that one might conform to negative stereotypes about their group. This phenomenon disproportionately affects underrepresented groups in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) fields—such as women and racial minorities—creating a self-fulfilling cycle of anxiety and reduced performance.
